title: Study Schedule Maker
description: Create a personalized study schedule for upcoming exams based on available time and difficulty
viralHook: Finals week panic? Build a study plan in 60 seconds flat

props.title: Study Schedule Maker
props.description: Enter your exam details and availability to generate a structured study plan. The schedule allocates more time to harder subjects and builds in review sessions.

fields.label: Exam Date
fields.helperText: The date of your first exam or the target date to be fully prepared by

fields.label: Subjects to Study
fields.helperText: Select all subjects you need to study for

fields.label: Hours Available per Day
fields.helperText: Be realistic — include only focused study time, not breaks

options.label: Light Review - I know most of the material
Moderate - Need to review key concepts
Deep Focus (50 min focus / 10 min break)

fields.label: Include Break Reminders
fields.helperText: Add scheduled breaks and self-care reminders to your plan

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any specific topics to prioritize, schedule constraints (e.g., 'No studying on Sundays', 'Weak in organic chemistry'), or other preferences...

props.outputTemplate:
Study Plan for {{exam_name}}

Exam Date: {{exam_date}}
Subjects: {{subjects}}
Daily Study Time: {{hours_per_day}} hours
Difficulty: {{difficulty}}
Study Method: {{study_style}}

--- RECOMMENDED SCHEDULE ---

Week Structure:
- Allocate {{hours_per_day}} hours daily across selected subjects
- Difficulty-weighted distribution: harder subjects get 40% more time
- Final 2 days reserved for comprehensive review
- Include practice tests where available

Daily Template:
1. Warm-up review of yesterday's material (15 min)
2. Primary subject block (40% of time)
3. Secondary subject block (30% of time)
4. Tertiary subject / weak areas (20% of time)
5. End-of-day summary and next-day planning (10% of time)

Tips for {{difficulty}} preparation:
- Light: Focus on practice problems and edge cases
- Moderate: Re-read notes, then do practice problems
- Heavy: Re-learn core concepts first, then practice
- Intensive: Start with fundamentals, build up gradually
